title.source: Map consultations: tapping into your community’s geospatial intelligence
title.target: Map consultations: tapping into your community’s geospatial intelligence

description.source: Understand our different mapping tools in the open and in surveys to elevate mobility and urban planning consultation
description.target: Understand our different mapping tools in the open and in surveys to elevate mobility and urban planning consultation

body:0002.source: Map consultations allow residents to share ideas, feedback, and data directly on a map, making community input more precise and visual.
body:0002.target: Map consultations allow residents to share ideas, feedback, and data directly on a map, making community input more precise and visual.

body:0003.source: They’re especially valuable for urban planning and mobility teams, who need to understand how people use and experience spaces.
body:0003.target: They’re especially valuable for urban planning and mobility teams, who need to understand how people use and experience spaces.

body:0004.source: The platform supports two map consultation methods:
body:0004.target: The platform supports two map consultation methods:

body:0005.source: Ideation on a Map – participants publicly pin and discuss ideas.
body:0005.target: Ideation on a Map – participants publicly pin and discuss ideas.

body:0006.source: Map‑based Survey Toolbox – admins collect structured inputs via pins, lines, or polygons inside surveys.
body:0006.target: Map‑based Survey Toolbox – admins collect structured inputs via pins, lines, or polygons inside surveys.

body:0007.source: By default, these methods use the platform’s built‑in OpenStreetMap‑based maps.
body:0007.target: By default, these methods use the platform’s built‑in OpenStreetMap‑based maps.

body:0008.source: ESRI Integration is an optional paid add‑on that replaces the default basemap and allows richer GIS layers and data to be displayed. If you want to enable ESRI, contact your government success manager to discuss this feature.
body:0008.target: ESRI Integration is an optional paid add‑on that replaces the default basemap and allows richer GIS layers and data to be displayed. If you want to enable ESRI, contact your government success manager to discuss this feature.

body:0010.source: Allows participants to drop pins and add comments on a shared, public map.
body:0010.target: Allows participants to drop pins and add comments on a shared, public map.

body:0012.source: Interactive pinning: Mark locations for projects, improvements, or issues
body:0012.target: Interactive pinning: Mark locations for projects, improvements, or issues

body:0013.source: Detailed comments: Add context to each pin
body:0013.target: Detailed comments: Add context to each pin

body:0014.source: Open discussion: See other pins and collaborate in real time
body:0014.target: Open discussion: See other pins and collaborate in real time

body:0015.source: Geographical context: Identify trends and hotspots visually
body:0015.target: Geographical context: Identify trends and hotspots visually

body:0016.source: How to style and configure maps for input collection
body:0016.target: How to style and configure maps for input collection

body:0019.source: Contact support to change the base map via MapTiler (affects all projects).
body:0019.target: Contact support to change the base map via MapTiler (affects all projects).

body:0020.source: To style maps per project, use the ESRI add‑on.
body:0020.target: To style maps per project, use the ESRI add‑on.

body:0023.source: Give each layer a name & optional tooltip.
body:0023.target: Give each layer a name & optional tooltip.

body:0024.source: Style by color and icon (applies to all features in the layer).
body:0024.target: Style by color and icon (applies to all features in the layer).

body:0025.source: 💡 create separate GeoJSON files for features you want styled differently.
body:0025.target: 💡 create separate GeoJSON files for features you want styled differently.

body:0026.source: ⚠️ Make sure your GeoJSON uses the correct coordinate system (WGS 84 / EPSG:4326, longitude–latitude order). Using another CRS may cause your features to appear in the wrong location.
body:0026.target: ⚠️ Make sure your GeoJSON uses the correct coordinate system (WGS 84 / EPSG:4326, longitude–latitude order). Using another CRS may cause your features to appear in the wrong location.

body:0027.source: Convert or create GeoJSON files if needed
body:0027.target: Convert or create GeoJSON files if needed

body:0028.source: Convert shapefiles or other formats → GeoJSON (e.g. mapshaper, geojson.io).
body:0028.target: Convert shapefiles or other formats → GeoJSON (e.g. mapshaper, geojson.io).

body:0029.source: Create your own GeoJSONs from scratch using tools like Geoman or directly in https://geojson.io/.
body:0029.target: Create your own GeoJSONs from scratch using tools like Geoman or directly in https://geojson.io/.

body:0033.source: Save position directly from map view.
body:0033.target: Save position directly from map view.

body:0035.source: Adds structured geospatial data collection to surveys.
body:0035.target: Adds structured geospatial data collection to surveys.

body:0037.source: Pin‑in‑map questions: Participants mark exact points; integrated heat map shows clusters
body:0037.target: Pin‑in‑map questions: Participants mark exact points; integrated heat map shows clusters

body:0038.source: Line/polygon drawing: Outline routes, boundaries, or areas of interest
body:0038.target: Line/polygon drawing: Outline routes, boundaries, or areas of interest

body:0039.source: Map layout pages: Embed maps as context for survey questions
body:0039.target: Map layout pages: Embed maps as context for survey questions

body:0040.source: Reporting: View aggregated results or individual responses; export as GeoJSON for external GIS tools
body:0040.target: Reporting: View aggregated results or individual responses; export as GeoJSON for external GIS tools

body:0042.source:
All responses to mapping questions are automatically displayed on a map.
For Drop pin questions, you can enable the Heat Map toggle to quickly see where most respondents placed their pins.
body:0042.target:
All responses to mapping questions are automatically displayed on a map.
For Drop pin questions, you can enable the Heat Map toggle to quickly see where most respondents placed their pins.

body:0044.source: To explore individual responses and link them to other survey answers:
body:0044.target: To explore individual responses and link them to other survey answers:

body:0045.source: Open the AI Analysis Interface by clicking Show AI Insight next to any Short Answer or Long Answer question.
body:0045.target: Open the AI Analysis Interface by clicking Show AI Insight next to any Short Answer or Long Answer question.

body:0046.source: If you don’t see this option, it means the feature isn’t included in your current plan.
body:0046.target: If you don’t see this option, it means the feature isn’t included in your current plan.

body:0047.source: ⚠️ You must include at least one Short Answer or Long Answer question in your survey to access the AI Analysis Interface.
body:0047.target: ⚠️ You must include at least one Short Answer or Long Answer question in your survey to access the AI Analysis Interface.

body:0052.source: ESRI Shapefile uploads (red): simplified outlines of uploaded geographic data
body:0052.target: ESRI Shapefile uploads (red): simplified outlines of uploaded geographic data

body:0053.source: You can filter responses based on answers to other survey questions, allowing you to focus on specific groups of participants.
body:0053.target: You can filter responses based on answers to other survey questions, allowing you to focus on specific groups of participants.

body:0054.source: ℹ️ The ESRI Shapefile visualisation in the AI interface shows simplified shapes for clarity. To explore detailed geographic data (including colours and textures), open the shapefile in an external mapping tool such as Mapshaper.
body:0054.target: ℹ️ The ESRI Shapefile visualisation in the AI interface shows simplified shapes for clarity. To explore detailed geographic data (including colours and textures), open the shapefile in an external mapping tool such as Mapshaper.

body:0056.source:
The AI Analysis Interface is not designed for deep geographic filtering or pattern discovery.
For more advanced geospatial analysis (e.g. filtering, combining, or comparing areas), export the shapefiles by pressing Expore as GeoJson in the top right corner of the Aggregated Map View in the survey Insights tab and use third-party tools like Mapshaper or your preferred GIS software.
body:0056.target:
The AI Analysis Interface is not designed for deep geographic filtering or pattern discovery.
For more advanced geospatial analysis (e.g. filtering, combining, or comparing areas), export the shapefiles by pressing Expore as GeoJson in the top right corner of the Aggregated Map View in the survey Insights tab and use third-party tools like Mapshaper or your preferred GIS software.

body:0058.source: Precise data collection: Inputs tied to exact locations
body:0058.target: Precise data collection: Inputs tied to exact locations

body:0059.source: Better visualization: Trends and hotspots become clear
body:0059.target: Better visualization: Trends and hotspots become clear

body:0060.source: Flexible results viewing: View results on one map or user‑by‑user; export for external GIS work
body:0060.target: Flexible results viewing: View results on one map or user‑by‑user; export for external GIS work

body:0061.source: Supports better planning: Helps teams make informed, data‑driven urban planning decisions
body:0061.target: Supports better planning: Helps teams make informed, data‑driven urban planning decisions

body:0063.source: The ESRI add‑on allows admins to integrate ArcGIS maps into projects for richer data and visualization.
body:0063.target: The ESRI add‑on allows admins to integrate ArcGIS maps into projects for richer data and visualization.

body:0065.source: Replace the default OpenStreetMap style with custom ESRI maps.
body:0065.target: Replace the default OpenStreetMap style with custom ESRI maps.

body:0066.source: Add Feature Layers and Web Maps to display detailed spatial data.
body:0066.target: Add Feature Layers and Web Maps to display detailed spatial data.

body:0067.source: Use ESRI maps in both Collect Input & Feedback (map view) and map‑based survey questions.
body:0067.target: Use ESRI maps in both Collect Input & Feedback (map view) and map‑based survey questions.

body:0069.source: ESRI Feature Layer – A single data layer (e.g., zoning, parks). Multiple layers can be added on the same map.
body:0069.target: ESRI Feature Layer – A single data layer (e.g., zoning, parks). Multiple layers can be added on the same map.

body:0070.source: ESRI Web Map – A fully configured ArcGIS map (base map + layers). Only one can be used per map.
body:0070.target: ESRI Web Map – A fully configured ArcGIS map (base map + layers). Only one can be used per map.

body:0071.source: ℹ️ Once ESRI is enabled on a map, GeoJSON imports are disabled — you can’t mix ESRI and non‑ESRI elements.
body:0071.target: ℹ️ Once ESRI is enabled on a map, GeoJSON imports are disabled — you can’t mix ESRI and non‑ESRI elements.

body:0073.source: Purchase the ESRI add‑on by contacting your government success manager
body:0073.target: Purchase the ESRI add‑on by contacting your government success manager

body:0075.source: Paste your ESRI API key (get it from ESRI documentation) and Save key
body:0075.target: Paste your ESRI API key (get it from ESRI documentation) and Save key

body:0077.source: Feature Layer: In back office map config, click Import ESRI Feature Layer → paste the Feature Layer URL from ArcGIS → Import
body:0077.target: Feature Layer: In back office map config, click Import ESRI Feature Layer → paste the Feature Layer URL from ArcGIS → Import

body:0078.source: Web Map: Click Import ESRI Web Map → paste the Web Map ID from ArcGIS → Import
body:0078.target: Web Map: Click Import ESRI Web Map → paste the Web Map ID from ArcGIS → Import

body:0079.source: Legend for Feature Layers is auto‑generated but can be toggled off.
body:0079.target: Legend for Feature Layers is auto‑generated but can be toggled off.

body:0081.source: When adding a mapping question in a survey, click Configure Map to add ESRI layers or a Web Map to the question’s map.
body:0081.target: When adding a mapping question in a survey, click Configure Map to add ESRI layers or a Web Map to the question’s map.

body:0083.source: Both ESRI and the platform have “click” features (e.g., clicking a shape to show info). If both try to respond at once, it can cause:
body:0083.target: Both ESRI and the platform have “click” features (e.g., clicking a shape to show info). If both try to respond at once, it can cause:

body:0084.source: Wrong info showing, pop‑ups failing, or map freezing.
body:0084.target: Wrong info showing, pop‑ups failing, or map freezing.

body:0085.source: Solution: Disable ESRI’s “on‑click” features and let the platform handle clicks for a smoother user experience.
body:0085.target: Solution: Disable ESRI’s “on‑click” features and let the platform handle clicks for a smoother user experience.
